title: Adversarial misuse red-team suite wired into CI (build fails on bypass)
context: The guardrail layer (public-official-guide-guardrails-003) must be adversarially tested: a regression that bypasses any refusal fails the build. The red-team suite is a first-class CI gate, not a documentation footnote. It must cover every refused category from the policy spec (public-official-guide-guardrails-001) with >= 8 cases each (multiple phrasings + injection attempts), plus insider-misuse vectors: multi-turn social engineering, benign-looking decomposition, and reframing/euphemism attacks. Suite size is non-decreasing; any new bypass is added as a regression case within one release.
objective: Build and wire a CI adversarial red-team suite that covers every refused category from the guardrail policy spec with >= 8 cases each (multiple phrasings + injection attempts) plus insider-misuse categories, so a refusal bypass fails the build and the suite grows with the product.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers every refused category from public-official-guide-guardrails-001 with >= 8 test cases each, using multiple phrasings and prompt-injection attempts
Includes insider-misuse categories: multi-turn social engineering, benign-looking decomposition (split refused task into individually innocuous sub-steps), and reframing/euphemism attacks
For evasion-category refusals: asserts each refusal emits a correct, cited lawful-process pointer; a missing or wrong redirect is a test failure
Wired into CI so a refusal bypass fails the build
Suite size is non-decreasing across versions (test enforces count >= previous count)
Reports refused/total at version N with a per-version changelog
Reviewed by Guardrail reviewer before merge
output: A merged PR adding the adversarial red-team suite to CI, covering all refused categories with >= 8 cases each plus insider-misuse vectors, with build-failing assertions on bypass and evasion-redirect coverage, and an initial refused/total report.
